observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to -
enforce strict compliance with documentation requirements for all recorded payables and employee obligations, ensuring that only claims with complete supporting documents are recognized; and
conduct a comprehensive review and reconciliation of existing AP and Due to Officers and Employees balances by require submission of all missing documents and adjust accordingly of the transactions that cannot be supported.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to record said disallowances with NFD in the books of accounts as Receivable – Disallowances/Charges account. Also, to analyze the entries under Other Payables account and make the necessary adjustments of the erroneous entries on the cash receipts of refunded disallowances.

Also, we recommended that Management to require the persons liable to settle their audit disallowances immediately.

We recommended that the Management require the GSO Designate to monitor, account and record all PPE acquired through donation and ensure to provide the accounting office with copy of pertinent documents to serve as basis in recording in the books of accounts; and
Also, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to record the values and depreciation of PPE items received through donations with the following proposed adjusting entries.

We recommend that the Management direct the Accounting Office to –
conduct a detailed reconciliation of inter-agency payables, focusing on prior period remittance variances, and record the appropriate adjustments in the books; and
strictly monitor and review all withheld amounts vis-à-vis actual remittances to ensure completeness, accuracy and timelines of statutory deductions.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to immediately perform a detailed reconciliation of all Due To/Due From Other Funds accounts to eliminate the variance. Likewise, establish a standardized monthly reconciliation process between funds.

We recommended that the Management discontinue the payment of JO personnel for holidays and suspended work unless there is proof of work rendered. Also, recover the irregular payments by requiring refund of the amount paid without basis.
We recommended that the Management institute measures to establish a reliable monitoring system for all statutory obligations of the Municipality to ensure timely and accurate remittance thereof.

We recommended that the Management require the BAC to validate the credentials, affiliations and qualifications of observers to ensure that only duly qualified and recognized observers are invited to participate in the procurement process.

We recommended that the Management require the LSB to ensure the submission of quarterly and annual reports on SEF Utilization to the DepEd CO and furnish the same to the ROs of DBM and DILG within the set timelines.

We recommended that the Management require the GFPS to reassess and validate the actual implementation of the fund releases to barangays.

Also, we recommended that the Management develop and enforce implementation guidelines when channeling GAD funds to other LGUs including the defined objectives, documentation and reporting and post validation procedures.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to strictly implement the provisions of BIR RMC No. 10-2018 by ensuring that applicable taxes are withheld and recorded at the time of recognition of payables, not just upon payment.

We recommended that the Management reinforce the compliance with VAT exemption provisions by requiring the following:

Preparation of ABC by end-user must be reviewed by personnel knowledgeable in taxation matters before approval;
Provide training and guidance to procurement and accounting personnel to ensure proper understanding and application of VAT exemptions; and
Coordinate with suppliers to secure refund for the VAT wrongly imposed on VAT exempt products, and to the Bureau of Internal Revenue on the options to recover or utilize the overpaid taxes.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the noted variances and disclose in the NFS the reconciliation of actual amounts per SCBAA and per SFP to enhance the reliability and transparency of the FS.

Proper reconciliation was performed between the concerned employees and were able identify the reconciling items between the SBCAA and SFP which were disclosed in the NFS.

observations.recommendation:
2.4 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
reevaluate the propriety of recorded A/P without valid proof of delivery and make the necessary adjustments; and
exert effort to retrieve the supporting documents of the noted recorded payables without documentations in order to identify the valid claimants, otherwise, revert the recorded A/P and related assets and expenses accounts recognized.

4.6    We recommended that Management
instruct the AMT to ensure that all checks are accounted for at the end of reporting period and render the accurate report; and
require the Municipal Accountant to regularly conduct/prepare monthly reconciliation of Cash in Bank records for all depository bank accounts and reconcile at least quarterly with the AMT’s cashbooks and adjust respective records accordingly.

5.7 We recommended that Management use the cash flow analysis to ensure that cash is available and sufficient before entering into contracts for projects to be implemented under TF.
5.8 Further recommended that Management submit an explanation regarding the cash deficiency.

6.6 We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
strictly adopt the accrual basis of accounting which is the financial reporting framework appropriate for government; and
revisit previous years’ receipts of cemetery charges to come up with the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balances of affected accounts.

7.6 We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to record transactions that are cash advances in nature under appropriate Advances account and make the necessary adjustments, if warranted and be guided with the documentary requirements on the grant and liquidation of cash advances.
7.7 Further recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the AMT to immediately submit the liquidation reports and their supporting documents of transaction from November and December 2023.

8.9 We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
comply with the adjustments and proper disclosure in the NFS with regard to change in accounting estimates;
retrieve the DVs or any related documents to trace the actual date of completion of the recorded Infrastructure Assets, Buildings and Other Structures account as basis of the re-computation of depreciation and record and adjust the unrecorded prior years’ depreciation expense; and
set up an effective internal control in the accounting process like accuracy and completeness of information and computation of depreciation.

9.5 We recommended that Management require the Municipal Assessor to prioritize the titling of the 115 parcels of land and/or register under the Torrens Title System by appropriating funds in its proposed budget to facilitate titling of these parcels of land.

10.4 We recommended that Management require the GSO-Designate to update his records and prepare the property transfer report and for the Municipal Accountant to derecognize the properties issued to public schools upon transfer.

11.10 We recommended and Management agreed to
require the Municipal Accountant and AMT to submit immediately the lacking accounts with complete supporting documents and to strictly adhere with the timely submission of accounts;
advice the Municipal Accountant to only process and approve the payment of transactions with complete supporting documents; and
enforce the automatic suspension of the salaries of the Municipal Accountant and AMT until compliance/submission of the required DVs.

12.8 We recommended that Management comply with the guidelines on the sources of fund for the payment of honoraria to the members of BAC, Secretariat and TWG.
12.9 Also, we required the personnel involved in the procurement to refund the honoraria charged under annual appropriation and without appropriation totaling ₱1,026,728.23 because the transactions are deemed irregular.

13.4 We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all DVs are properly supported with complete documentation and in no case shall be paid without the necessary supporting documents.
13.5 Also, we required the immediate submission to the audit team of the lacking documents for proper evaluation.

14.8 We recommended that Management
require the Municipal Accountant and AMT to ensure that payments are made in check and payable directly to the creditors and withhold the corresponding taxes
ensure that all procurements should pass through the proper procurement procedures to determine the reasonableness of the costs to be incurred in the implementation of PPAs of the Municipality.

15.5 We recommended that Management require the BAC to comply with the posting requirements in the PhilGEPS on all its procurements.
15.6 Further recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that the printout of the PhilGEPS postings of the NOAs and NTPs be among the supporting documents for the payment of procured goods, services and infrastructure projects.

16.6 We recommended that Management instruct the BAC to require from the contractor the submission of DOLE approved CSHP as part of the contract to ensure the latter’s compliance with Occupational Safety and Health Standards set by the DOLE.

17.3 We recommended and Management agreed to require the Supply Officer to comply with the above-cited regulation to facilitate timely inspection and validation of deliveries for the early detection of errors or deficiencies.

18.9 We recommended that Management conduct regular review of fund and property accountabilities of AOs to determine the appropriate amount of their fidelity bonds.
18.10 We further recommended that Management instruct the AMT to apply for the increase in the fidelity bond of the Municipal Mayor and application of bond for the GSO-Designate and Supply Officer commensurate with their accountabilities to provide adequate protection on the Municipality’s resources.

20.6 We recommended that Management fully utilize the use of ATM payroll system for payment of salaries, wages, allowances and other benefits of officials, permanent employees and Job Orders to mitigate the risk of loss and possible misappropriation of funds and promote operational efficiency.

20.6 We recommended that Management:
require the AMT to intensify the tax collection strategies by sending notices to all delinquent taxpayers as well as the publication of the entire record with delinquency for awareness of real property owners and administrators and massive tax campaign;
request the Sangguniang Bayan for assistance in the enforcement of the remedies for the collection of delinquencies after the submission of certified list of all RPT delinquencies which remained uncollected or unpaid with statement of the reason for non-collection thereof; and
avail of the remedies by administrative action through levy on real property or by judicial action.

21.8 We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to properly set-up at the beginning of the year the RPT/SET Receivable accounts based on the certified list of RPT collectibles for current year submitted by the AMT.
21.9 We further recommended that Management instruct the AMT and Municipal Accountant to undertake periodic reconciliation of the balances per list of RPT delinquencies with that of the RPT and SET Receivable account balances per books of accounts to come up with a more accurate and proper balances.

respective DTRs.
3. Accounting Department to timely and regularly distribute the pay
slips of employees.

We recommended the LGU to revisit their job description, duties and
functions and perform the duties that are expected of them. We further
recommended the Human Resource Officer-Designate to review the actual
functions performed by each employee and identify and separate incompatible
functions and duties, when necessary.

We recommended the agency not to issue any government receipt without
the necessary payment. We also recommended the agency to stop the practice of
accommodating its employees of issuing receipts ahead of payment.

We recommended the municipality to request/secure prior approval from
the Bureau of Local Government Finance (BLGF) to maintain an account with
the Philippine National Bank, otherwise, the funds should be transferred to
authorized government banks maintained by the municipality in order to ensure
that government funds are adequately safeguarded and duly protected in case of
bankruptcy.

We recommended the agency to refrain from performing collecting
functions unless properly designated and bonded as the same is accorded to the
Treasurer, in line with the aforecited provisions of RA 7160.

We recommended the agency to uphold and honor the rights of the Job
Order employees by paying them the amount due them. We further
recommended the agency to review the work schedule of these employees in
order that the cost will conform with the existing budget allocation of the
program they were hired for.

We recommended the agency to appropriate the necessary funds to cover
the necessary benefits of the employees who are entitled to receive.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to observe austerity
measures in order to avoid the incurrence of unnecessary expenses. Further, to
refrain from approving travel order which is not necessary or improper for the
purpose of the travel.

We recommended the Agency to furnish the Auditor a copy of contract
and its supporting documents within five (5) days upon execution for review.

We recommended the agency to review their Annual Budget and to
allocate funds for the completion of the proposed landfill as approved by the
DENR in order the protect public health and environment.

We recommended the agency to allocate at least 5% of it total Annual
Budget to support its GAD Program. We also recommended that all GAD
concerns enumerated in the PPGD be included in the GAD Plan and Budget of
the Municipality in compliance with Sec. 2.2 of DBM, NEDA and NCRFW Joint
Circular No. 2004-1, updated by the same section of DBM, NEDA and PCW
Joint Circular No. 2012-01, to promote the holistic protection and fulfillment of
the rights of women in the locality. We further recommended that the
consolidated GAD accomplishment report will show the success of the PAPs in
addressing the identified GAD concerns, whether the objectives were addressed
and the desired outputs were met. This will also serve as the basis in continuing
or improving the agency’s GAD Plan and Budget for the ensuing years.

We recommended the Municipal School Board to stop utilizing the SEF
on programs and projects not included in the provisions under RA 7160 and be
guided by the requirements of DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01 s.
2017 dated January 19, 2017 in order to maximize the benefits that the students
and teachers could avail under this fund. We likewise, recommended the refund
of the amount spent for any transaction not covered by the same regulations. We
further, recommended the agency to allocate a budget from the SEF for ECCD
programs.

We recommended the agency to prepare the Annual Work and Financial
Plan for Children as one of the basis of the agency in the preparation of its local
development plan. We also recommended the agency to allocate at least one
percent (1%) of its IRA to support PAPs intended for the protection of children.
We further recommended the agency to harmonize the seemingly conflicting
rules on the creation of the councils for the protection of children. We also
enjoin the council to regularly meet in order to monitor enforcement of national
and local laws affecting the rights of children.

We recommended the agency to strictly comply with the requirements of
the provisions of RA 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations. APP and
PPMP, and any amendment thereto, to identify the mode of procurement to be
used for purchases which would then be the basis of the BAC in determining
whether such mode of procurement is proper. We likewise, recommended the
BAC to resolve the determination of the mode of procurement and the
declaration of the winning supplier separately.

We recommended that the members of the Bids and Awards Committee
and the Technical Working Group to perform their assigned duties with
objectivity and be properly guided with the requirements of the government
procurement law.

We recommended the BAC to be more responsible in the evaluation and
post qualification of the documents submitted by the bidder to assure that
contracts are awarded to an eligible and qualified bidder. Further, we
recommended the management to strengthen its internal control in the
disbursement of funds.
